skill.name: notion-spec-to-implementation
skill.description: Guides an agent through converting a Notion PRD or feature spec into an implementation plan, linked Notion tasks, and progress updates using Notion MCP.
skill.summary: Convert Notion specs into implementation plans, linked tasks, and progress tracking.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak cryptography, network, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. AI review found no executable code or scripts; most hits are Markdown inline tool and file references, and the only network endpoint is the official Notion MCP setup URL. The skill can create or update Notion pages after OAuth, so publish as low risk with clear permission expectations.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The static external command hits are Markdown inline code references to Notion MCP tools, local reference filenames, examples, and setup commands. They do not execute Ruby or shell code by themselves. Lines 23, 25, and 27 are user-visible Codex setup commands that require explicit user action.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown, not Ruby, and the backticked strings are rendered as inline documentation. The only shell-like entries are setup commands shown to the user, not automatically executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: Official Notion MCP Endpoint Requires Network Access
low_findings.description: Line 23 documents adding the Notion MCP server at https://mcp.notion.com/mcp. This is expected for a Notion workflow, but it means the skill depends on an external service and OAuth-granted Notion permissions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is visible and points to the Notion MCP endpoint described by the skill workflow. No evidence shows covert network access or an unrelated third-party endpoint.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography findings at lines 3 and 5 occur in descriptive metadata about implementation plans and progress tracking. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are YAML front matter description text. I found no code, dependency, or instruction that uses MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Hit Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance finding at line 51 maps to the Track progress section heading. The surrounding instructions discuss status updates, milestone summaries, checklists, blockers, and decisions, not scanning or probing networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is project progress tracking. No evidence found for port scanning, host discovery, DNS enumeration, or other reconnaissance behavior.

content.user_title: Convert Notion Specs into Implementation Workflows
content.value_statement: Teams often lose detail when a Notion PRD becomes engineering work. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through plans, tasks, links, and status updates in Notion.
actual_capabilities: Guides discovery and fetching of a Notion specification through Notion MCP tools.
Directs extraction of requirements, acceptance criteria, constraints, priorities, and assumptions.
Defines when to create a quick plan versus a standard multi-phase implementation plan.
Describes how to create linked Notion task pages with database properties and acceptance criteria.
Provides a cadence for updating implementation status, blockers, decisions, and milestone summaries.
limitations: Requires a connected Notion MCP server and OAuth permissions before Notion actions can run.
Referenced reference and example files are not present in this package, so detailed templates are unavailable.
Does not implement code changes by itself; it organizes implementation work in Notion.
Task quality depends on the completeness and clarity of the original Notion specification.

use_cases.title: Turn a PRD into engineering tasks
use_cases.description: Convert a Notion PRD into a plan page and clear task pages for engineering execution.

use_cases.title: Plan a multi-phase feature build
use_cases.description: Break a complex feature or migration into phases, dependencies, risks, and trackable milestones.

use_cases.title: Maintain implementation status
use_cases.description: Keep the spec, plan, task pages, blockers, decisions, and progress summaries aligned in Notion.

prompt_templates.title: Start from a Notion spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the notion-spec-to-implementation skill to find my Notion spec named [spec title], summarize requirements, and list any clarifying questions before creating work items.

prompt_templates.title: Create a quick implementation plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the notion-spec-to-implementation skill for [Notion spec link]. Create a concise implementation plan with scope, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and a small task list.

prompt_templates.title: Build a full delivery plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the notion-spec-to-implementation skill for [Notion spec link]. Build a phased implementation plan, identify risks, and create linked tasks sized for one to two days each.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and update progress
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the notion-spec-to-implementation skill to review the plan and task pages for [project]. Update progress, blockers, decisions, and milestone status without changing unrelated Notion pages.

output_examples.input: A Notion PRD for a new dashboard filter feature.
output_examples.output: A linked implementation plan with requirements summary, assumptions, phases, dependencies, risks, success criteria, and task pages for design, API, UI, tests, and release.

output_examples.input: A database migration spec with unclear rollout steps.
output_examples.output: A standard plan that highlights clarifying questions, separates preparation and rollout phases, and creates tasks for migration checks, validation, fallback planning, and status updates.

output_examples.input: An existing plan with several open tasks and blockers.
output_examples.output: A progress update that synchronizes task status, records blockers and decisions, and summarizes milestone completion for the plan page.

best_practices: Confirm the selected Notion spec and task database before creating or updating pages.
Resolve major requirement gaps and assumptions before generating a full task set.
Keep generated tasks small, linked to the source spec, and tied to clear acceptance criteria.
anti_patterns: Do not authorize Notion MCP access unless the workspace and requested permissions are expected.
Do not create tasks from an ambiguous spec without recording clarifying questions and assumptions.
Do not update unrelated Notion pages or databases while tracking implementation progress.

faq.question: Does this skill require Notion MCP?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed around Notion MCP search, fetch, page creation, and page update tools.

faq.question: Can it create Notion tasks automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide task creation through Notion MCP after the user grants access and confirms the correct database.

faq.question: Does it write application code?
faq.answer: No. It turns specs into plans, tasks, links, and progress updates for implementation work.

faq.question: What should I check before using it?
faq.answer: Confirm the source spec, Notion workspace, task database schema, required properties, and OAuth permissions.

faq.question: Are the referenced templates included?
faq.answer: No. The package only includes SKILL.md, so referenced template and example files are not available here.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with low risk. The main consideration is authorized Notion network access and page mutation.
